topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic stick felt cold against my palm, the results glowing a stark, undeniable truth. My breath hitched, a shallow, ragged thing in the quiet of my bedroom. I stared at the screen, the unfamiliar names swimming before my eyes, each one a tiny anchor dragging me under. My hands started to tremble, the fine motor skills failing me as I tried to process the sheer impossibility of it. A wave of nausea rolled over me, making the room tilt slightly. I sank onto the edge of the bed, pulling my knees up to my chest, wishing the floor would just swallow me whole. The silence in the house suddenly amplified, each tick of the clock a hammer blow against my composure.

[story 2]

He looked at the printout, his expression unreadable, but the sudden stillness in his posture spoke volumes. "This is... complicated," he finally managed, his voice a low rasp. I couldn't meet his gaze; my eyes were fixed on the paper, tracing the lines of shared heritage that suddenly felt like a heavy, unwelcome inheritance. A tightness constricted my throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I hugged my arms around myself, a small, futile attempt to shield the exposed core of my chest. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to spring away from the shock, yet frozen in place by an overwhelming sense of disorientation.

[story 3]

The lab report sat on the kitchen counter, mocking me with its clinical precision. I picked up the paper, the edges crinkling under my fingers, and felt a sudden, sharp sting behind my eyes. It wasn't just the information; it was the sudden realization that the foundation of my life, the very soil I thought I stood on, was built on shifting sand. I walked to the window, pressing my forehead against the cool glass, trying to anchor myself to the distant, indifferent skyline. A tremor ran through my hands, and I squeezed them into fists until the skin felt stretched tight, fighting the urge to simply collapse onto the hardwood floor.
